method: QE tot_charge jellium scan (negative tot_charge = ADD electrons + compensating uniform background); nspin=2, starting_magnetization(Co)=0.3 diagnostic; ecutwfc=90 / ecutrho=360 Ry; 12x12x12 MP k-mesh (scf), Gamma-K-M-Gamma-A 12 pts/seg (bands, david diago_full_acc=.false.). NC ONCV PBE-sr pseudos (Co z=17, Sn z=14; 93 e-/cell undoped). Flat-band locator = flattest band within +-1.5 eV of E_F = band 44 (Co-3d kagome flat band).
hypothesis: Electron-doping CoSn by dn ~ +0.3-0.6 e-/f.u. brings the kagome flat band TO E_F (rigid-band slope +0.97 eV/d-electron from CoSn(-0.44,3d7) vs MoSn(-2.38,4d5)) while keeping it non-magnetic.

doping_scan.note: UNDOPED CONTROL — reproduces documented CoSn -0.44 eV (setup sane)
flat band DEEPER below E_F, not nearer

interpretation: Pre-registered gate (frozen, no goalpost move): PASS requires SOME dn in [0.2,0.8] with |dE|<0.10 eV AND m<0.5 uB. RESULT: the kagome Co-3d flat band (band 44) sits ~0.45 eV below E_F undoped and moves MONOTONICALLY DEEPER with electron doping (-0.445 -> -0.463 -> -0.511 -> -0.585 eV across dn=0,0.2,0.4,0.6; effective slope ~ -0.23 eV per added e-). This is the OPPOSITE of the rigid-band lead's +0.97 eV/d-electron prediction: self-consistently, electron doping fills DISPERSIVE bands above the flat band so E_F rises FASTER (16.648 -> 16.918 eV) than the flat-band manifold (16.203 -> 16.334 eV), WIDENING the gap. No doping in range gives |dE|<0.10 -> alignment FALSIFIED. HOWEVER the magnetism half PASSED strongly: m collapses 0.09 -> 0.00 uB by dn=0.2 and stays 0.00 -- electron doping DOES quench CoSn's weak itinerant magnetism (one of the two birds confirmed). Net: 🔴 FALSIFY on the joint gate (alignment fails), 🟢 on the magnetism axis only.
rigid_band_lead_assessment: The triangulation-v2 rigid-band slope (+0.97 eV/d-electron, N=2 from CoSn-vs-MoSn) is NOT predictive for in-situ doping of fixed-composition CoSn. The Co->Mo substitution slope conflates (a) changing the transition-metal d-band center (chemistry) with (b) changing electron count. tot_charge doping isolates (b) only, and (b) alone pushes E_F up through dispersive states, not the flat band toward E_F. The rigid-band guide is REFUTED as a doping dial for CoSn.
jellium_caveat: tot_charge doping adds a uniform compensating jellium background (rigid-band-with-screening proxy); it is NOT a real chemical dopant. It captures band-filling self-consistently but omits the dopant's own orbitals, local potential, and lattice change. A real substitutional dopant (CoSn1-xSbx electron-donor on Sn, or Co1-xNixSn) could shift the flat band differently via chemistry. Given the CLEAR monotone wrong-direction trend (0.45->0.59 eV over 0->0.6 e-), a substitutional supercell/VCA confirmation is NOT warranted for alignment -- the band-filling physics is unambiguous. (Would only revisit if a dopant-specific orbital resonance were proposed.)
pod: vast 41068077 (ssh2.vast.ai:28076, RTX-class host, 32 dedicated cores, Ubuntu 22.04 + apt quantum-espresso 6.7 + openmpi, np=32). DOWN after harvest.
evidence_cmd: per dn_X/: mpirun --allow-run-as-root -np 32 pw.x -in scf.in (nspin=2, tot_charge=-dn, conv 1e-10, converged 20-25 iters); pw.x -in bands.in (Gamma-K-M-Gamma-A, david diago_full_acc=.false.); bands.x -> cosn_bands.dat.gnu. Control scf.out: 'the Fermi energy is 16.6482 ev', 'total magnetization = 0.09 Bohr mag/cell'; flat band band 44 disp 0.188 eV mean 16.203 eV -> dE -0.445 eV (== documented -0.44). Harvest: scripts/scratch/qforge_harvest/cosn_edope/ (dn_{0.0,0.2,0.4,0.6}/{scf,bands}.{in,out}, cosn_bands.dat.gnu, parsed_scan.json).
recommended_next: CoSn electron-doping CLOSED-negative for flat-band alignment. (1) The magnetism-quench win means a NON-MAGNETIC CoSn-type with the flat band ALREADY near E_F is the target -- not doping CoSn. (2) Per deck fallback, gate-check LaRu3Si2 (Ru-kagome, nm, real Tc=7K, flat band expected nearer E_F) -- already QUEUED in ledger. (3) HOLE-doping CoSn (positive tot_charge) is the untested direction the data implies: if the flat band tracks BELOW E_F and E_F rises with electrons, then REMOVING electrons would lower E_F toward the flat band -- worth a cheap 2-point hole scan (tot_charge=+0.2,+0.4) before abandoning CoSn entirely.
honesty_notes: Real QE outputs (c2). Control reproduced -0.44 eV exactly -> setup verified. No fitting to the predicted -0.05 eV; the measured trend is monotone AWAY from E_F and reported as-is (d6/c9). Flat-band locator = flattest band in +-1.5 eV window, consistent band 44 at every doping (dispersion ~0.19 eV, well-resolved). Magnetization is the converged final SCF value, not a projection.
